title: Evidence-grounded staffing significance at a service cutoff
prompt: A supplied operations log documents that hourly staffing rosters are locked before a service window and records arrival counts, service times, wages, and waiting times for those windows. A queueing-based model selects integer staffing by hour to minimize expected labor and waiting cost. Frame why the decision is managerially significant using only the supplied operational evidence, without claiming causal savings or broader generalization.

model_specification.mathematical_objective: minimize expected labor cost plus waiting cost
constraints: roster locked before the service window
model_specification.mechanism: Scheduled staffing sets service capacity, which interacts with stochastic arrivals and service times to generate queues and waiting cost.
model_specification.uncertainty: arrivals and service times during the window

fidelity_requirements: Use the supplied log only to establish the recurring decision and measured burden
Do not turn modeled cost differences into causal realized savings
prohibited_inferences: The optimized roster has already reduced realized waiting or labor cost
Findings generalize beyond the logged service windows

evidence_needs.claim: Rosters are locked before the service window and waiting is observed in the supplied windows
The modeled roster will causally reduce realized cost
